channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Thousands of Palestinians return to what's left of their homes as Gaza ceasefire takes effect
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — Tens of thousands of Palestinians headed back to the heavily destroyed northern Gaza Strip on Friday as a U.S.-brokered ceasefire came into effect in a deal that raised hopes for ending the Israel-Hamas war. All the remaining hostages were set to be released within days.
item.dc:creator: ABDEL KAREEM HANA, WAFAA SHURAFA and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump to visit Egypt and Israel early next week amid ceasefire deal in Middle East
item.description: President Trump said Friday he plans to visit Egypt and Israel during a one-day trip to the Middle East early next week to mark the implementation of a peace agreement that will see a pause in fighting in Gaza and the release of dozens of hostages.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Trump announces 100 percent tariff on China in response to rare earth controls
item.description: President Trump announced Friday he will raise tariffs on China in response to a move from Beijing to tighten its control over certain critical minerals and rare earth elements. Trump announced on Truth Social that he would impose a tariff of 100 percent on Chinese goods beginning Nov. 1 or sooner.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize bets on Polymarket raise leak concerns, prompts probe
item.description: The last-minute bets, first reported by local outlets, are a cause for concern for the Norwegian officials in charge of awarding the honor
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: Dutch far-right politician Wilders fears he may have been a target of Belgian attack plotters
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Dutch far-right politician Geert Wilders said Friday that he’s suspending campaigning for national elections later this month following reports that he was a possible target of a suspected plot in Belgium to kill politicians using a drone carrying explosives.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Putin praises Trump's peace efforts despite failing to win the Nobel prize
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Friday praised U.S. President Donald Trump's peace efforts, despite not winning a Nobel, and voiced hope for securing an agreement to extend a pivotal nuclear arms pact with Washington for another year.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: With ceasefire in effect, thousands trek to northern Gaza
item.description: As thousands of displaced Palestinians celebrated the ceasefire, Gaza-based photojournalist Fadel Mghari captured images of their trek home.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, The Hill

item.title: Russian strikes wound at least 20 in Ukraine's capital as child is killed in separate attack
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian drone and missile strikes wounded at least 20 people in Kyiv, damaged residential buildings and caused blackouts across swaths of Ukraine early Friday, authorities said. A child also was killed in separate attacks in the southeast of the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republican leaders backed Venezuela's Machado for Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Sen. Rick Scott, R-Fla., signed a letter supporting opposition leader María Corina Machado.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: PHOTOS: With ceasefire in effect, thousands trek to northern Gaza
item.description: As thousands of displaced Palestinians celebrated the ceasefire, Gaza-based photojournalist Fadel Mghari captured images of their trek home.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, NewsNation

item.title: The Taliban government blames Pakistan for a strike on Kabul
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — The Taliban government on Friday blamed Pakistan for striking the Afghan capital and bombing a market in the country's east, accusing its neighbor of violating national airspace. A blast struck before 10 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Arctic seals, more than half of bird species on latest threatened list
item.description: While many animals are increasingly at risk of disappearing forever, the updated list shows how species can come back from the brink with dedicated effort.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel's military says Gaza ceasefire now in effect
item.description: Israel's military announced early Friday that the ceasefire in Gaza is now in effect, after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's Cabinet approved the first phase of President Trump's 20-point peace plan.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Israel-Hamas ceasefire takes effect, IDF begins pulling back in Gaza
item.description: Israeli troops began repositioning to pre-determined locations as the ceasefire with Hamas began, bringing the hostages one step closer to going home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Burkina Faso rejects proposal to accept deportees from the US
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Burkina Faso says it has refused a proposal from the Trump administration to accept deportees from the United States. The West African country was asked whether it would accept non-citizens expelled by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: India to upgrade Kabul mission to full embassy, signaling deeper ties with Taliban
item.description: SRINAGAR, India (AP) — India is upgrading its technical mission in Kabul to a full embassy, India’s foreign minister announced Friday after meeting his Afghanistan counterpart in New Delhi.
item.dc:creator: AIJAZ HUSSAIN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Opposition activist María Corina Machado of Venezuela wins the Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: The former opposition presidential candidate in Venezuela was lauded for being a “key, unifying figure in a political opposition that was once deeply divided — an opposition that found common ground in the demand for free elections and representative government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Citing unease over graft, Japan's Komeito leaves the longstanding ruling coalition headed by the LDP
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — The head of Japan's Komeito says it is leaving the ruling coalition headed by the Liberal Democratic Party due to concerns over corruption, in a major setback for the woman who hopes to become the country's next prime minister.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump doesn’t get Nobel Peace Prize; María Corina Machado awarded
item.description: President Trump failed in his bid to be awarded the Nobel Peace Prize, with the Norwegian Nobel committee announcing Friday that María Corina Machado was given this year’s award for promoting democratic rights for the people of Venezuela.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Taiwan's president pledges to build air defense system in face of China threat
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan will accelerate the building of a “Taiwan Shield” or “T-Dome” air defense system in the face of the military threat from China, its leader said Friday. President Lai Ching-te also pledged to raise defense spending to more than 3% of GDP and to reach 5% by 2030.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU and SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Macron is set to appoint a new French prime minister in last-ditch move to tackle turmoil
item.description: PARIS (AP) — After a week of intense political turmoil, French President Emmanuel Macron is set to appoint a new prime minister Friday in his latest bid to break the political deadlock that has gripped the country for more than a year, as France struggles with mounting economic challenges and balloo
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli Cabinet approves Trump's plan for Gaza ceasefire and release of hostages held by Hamas
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel’s Cabinet early Friday approved President Donald Trump’s plan for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of all the remaining hostages held by Hamas, a key step toward ending a ruinous two-year war that has destabilized the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, MELANIE LIDMAN and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Madagascar's Gen Z protesters clash with police as they call for president to step down
item.description: ANTANANARIVO, Madagascar (AP) — About 1,000 protesters marched in Madagascar's capital on Thursday, clashing with police who used tear gas and stun grenades to disperse them in the third week of what has been the most significant unrest in years in the Indian Ocean island nation.
item.dc:creator: SARAH TÉTAUD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Nobel Peace Prize nominations
item.description: Throughout his second term, President Trump has campaigned for the Nobel Peace Prize. The president, his allies and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu have renewed the push since Israel and Hamas agreed to the first phase of a peace plan Trump proposed last month.
item.dc:creator: Max Rego, The Hill

item.title: UN is ready to surge aid into Gaza and waiting for green light from Israel after deal
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United Nations said Thursday that 170,000 metric tons of food, medicine and other humanitarian aid is ready to enter Gaza and that it is seeking a green light from Israel to massively increase help for more than 2 million Palestinians following a deal to pause the war.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: From grief to gratitude: Trump praised as Israelis celebrate Hamas hostage deal in Tel Aviv
item.description: Israelis express joy, relief and grief at Hostages Square following President Donald Trump's announcement of agreement to end Israel-Hamas war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump pushing for drastic reduction in refugees allowed into US
item.description: "Our concern is that this could turn what has long been a globally responsive humanitarian system into one that overwhelmingly favors a single group.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Europe takes on 'hybrid war' as Germany allows police to shoot down drones
item.description: EU President Ursula von der Leyen warns Russia's 'hybrid warfare' targeting Europe through airspace violations and cyber attacks needs to be directly acknowledged.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Greta Thunberg slammed for using image of starved Israeli hostage to show Palestinians suffering in Gaza
item.description: Greta Thunberg is being criticized after using a photo of starving hostage Evyatar David in an Instagram post claiming to show Palestinians suffering.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Your phone rings, and it's a number from Sweden. Do you answer? A Nobel Prize winner didn't
item.description: This year’s Nobel Prize science winners got the good news from early morning phone calls and door knocks.
item.dc:creator: Stefanie Dazio And Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel seizes another Gaza flotilla
item.description: Israel intercepted another flotilla attempting to break the naval blockade and reach Gaza. This comes just days after another fleet was intercepted.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump thanks group representing families of hostages for Nobel Peace Prize appeal
item.description: President Donald Trump thanked the Hostages and Missing Families Forum for its appeal to the Nobel committee to award the president the Nobel Peace Prize.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cuban-led caravan aims for Mexico City as Trump policies deter migrants from US
item.description: Cuban migrants in Mexico choose Mexico City over U.S. destinations as President Donald Trump's strict immigration policies influence regional migration trends.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel and Hamas launch indirect talks in Egypt on eve of Gaza war anniversary
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israeli and Hamas officials launched indirect talks Monday at an Egyptian resort on a U.S.-drafted peace plan to end the ruinous war in Gaza on the eve of its second anniversary. The talks, which went on for several hours, unfolded amid many questions about the plan presented by U.S.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, MELANIE LIDMAN and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Big Time Rush's James Maslow releases new song celebrating Israel
item.description: Maslow acknowledged he chose Oct. 7th because of how the "country (Israel) and this world have normalized a hate towards each other.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, NewsNation

item.title: UK police given more time to question 4 suspects over deadly Manchester synagogue attack
item.description: LONDON (AP) — U.K. police were given extra time Saturday to question four people arrested on suspicion of terror offenses after an attack on a synagogue in northwest England that left two people dead and Britain’s Jewish community shocked and grieving.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation
